description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

Closes the architectural debt from `.13`s cooperative-cancel + Code.Server settle pass. `.13` mitigated the Code.Server lock symptom but left the supervision posture inline-and-unsupervised; this ticket structurally completes F2 and the consequent High findings.

Findings covered:
- **F2-arch** (CRIT consensus from 3 reviewers: Security, Distributed, Performance): `Task.async` + `Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill)` only kills the direct task pid; grandchildren spawned by killed tests (Phoenix.PubSub, Ecto pools, etc.) survive as orphans. `Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2` recursive-kills the full link tree.
- **F25** (HIGH): No `MutagenEx.Application`, no `mod:` in mix.exs. Library callers (anyone using `:mutagen_ex` as a dep) have no entry point.
- **F26** (HIGH): `:cover_server` and `ExUnit.Server` are singletons. Concurrent callers silently corrupt each others state. No ownership contract documented or enforced.
- **F46** (MEDIUM, partial from .13): brutal-kill leaves linked processes / sockets / ETS — snapshot detects but never reaps. With a supervisor, terminate_child propagates `:shutdown` through the link tree.

## Deliverable

- New `lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex` implementing `Application` with a one-for-one supervisor whose only child (initially) is a named `Task.Supervisor` registered as `MutagenEx.TaskSup`.
- `mix.exs`: add `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` to `application/0`.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex`: swap `Task.async` → `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink(MutagenEx.TaskSup, fn -> ... end)`. Swap `Task.shutdown` → `Task.Supervisor.terminate_child(MutagenEx.TaskSup, pid)`. Retain `.13`s `:code.purge` settle pass on top.
- Document and enforce the singleton-ownership contract for `:cover_server` and `ExUnit.Server`: while a MutagenEx run is in flight, `MutagenEx.TaskSup` is the documented owner; a second concurrent caller fails with a clear `{:error, :cover_already_running, ...}` (extending the existing r1 rejection in coverage_runner.ex:96-106).
- New decision file `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md` documenting the choice (and why `Task.Supervisor` instead of out-of-process workers as the v1 isolation primitive — out-of-process is captured as a Wont-Have / v2 design note).
- Revise `.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md`: new requirements for supervision-tree contract + recursive shutdown invariant.
- Revise `.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md`: new requirement for singleton ownership contract.
- New test file `test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs` exercising start_link / terminate cycles, recursive-kill propagation through a synthetic helper process tree, and concurrent-caller rejection.
- Update README: declare `MutagenEx.Application` as program entrypoint; remove the `.13`-residual caveat about "re-run if the tail looks wrong".

## Verification

- `mix test` green including new `supervision_test.exs`
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- Strict pre-merge gate green
- Spec verification stubs for the new supervision + singleton requirements pass
- The integration spike (c1_test/c2_test) still passes — the supervision swap must not regress baseline behavior

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex internals — `.17`s `with_restore/4` is the right wrapping shape; this ticket should not duplicate or refactor it
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex internals beyond the documented singleton-rejection extension
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex, test_selector.ex, baseline.ex, ast_cache.ex — AST/perf is `.25`
- Out-of-process workers / Port-based isolation (explicitly Wont-Have in the landing-plan spec)

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (new file)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (Task.Supervisor swap)
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (singleton-ownership rejection extension)
- mix.exs (mod: declaration)
- test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md (new)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope (intent)

- Do not introduce parallelism on the mutation loop in this ticket — having `Task.Supervisor` is the foundation; `.30` (F17) builds on it
- Do not change MutationLoop.run/1 outcome shape — internal mechanics swap but the tagged outcome tuples stay identical
- Do not change `restore/3` or `with_restore/4` (from `.17`)
- Cancellation-grace tuning (`cancel_grace_ms`, `Task.Supervisor` `:shutdown_timeout`) — pick a sensible default (existing 100 ms grace + 5_000 ms hard) and document; deeper tuning is `.30` territory

## Merge gate

- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] supervision_test.exs covers: start_link, recursive shutdown, concurrent-caller rejection
- [ ] Spec verification for new requirements pass
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] README + CHANGELOG updated
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE on this ticket

## Recommended planning path

This ticket is architectural enough to warrant /mega-plan in a fresh session before /implement (per the landing-plan spec). The kernel is in /Users/mz/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/plans/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen_ex-mutagen-wrd/2026-05-13-mutagen-wrd-review-fixes/00-spec.md.

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR NOTE — pre-existing format-gate red, NOT caused by 18.1

After 18.1 merged cleanly into epic/mutagen-wrd at f384c25 (smoke 357/357 green, compile clean, deps.unlock clean), the standard merge-gate `mix format --check-formatted` surfaced pre-existing formatting violations in:

- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs (4 sites: multi-line tuple literals)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (1 site: if/do/else single-line that should split)
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex (1 site: error-message wrap)

These violations exist on the pre-18.1 epic HEAD (55af051) and earlier — confirmed by `git checkout 55af051 -- <files> && mix format --check-formatted` showing the same diffs. 18.1 did not touch any of these files (its diff was only `lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex` NEW + one line in `mix.exs`).

None of the remaining children (18.2-18.5) have these files in their Allowed touches, so the epic cannot fix this debt without a scope expansion. Surfacing for human visibility; orchestrator proceeding with 18.2 + 18.3 in parallel. Address the format debt with a follow-on cleanup ticket or by running `mix format` on those three files outside this epic.

title: S9b: MutagenEx.Application + Task.Supervisor + singleton ownership contract
